t_ch: 家族在很久以前就将「钟表匠」视作敌人，但苦于后者神龙见首不见尾，只活在他一手缔造的商业神话中，猎犬们迟迟抓不到他。
t_en: The Family has considered the Watchmaker as an enemy for a long time, but the Hounds haven't been able to track him down, as he seems to be living only in the characters and stories he created.

t_ch: 所以，我进一步向各位提问，你们是否想过，为什么家族能容忍「钟表匠」向外界送出这种笑话一样的信息，任凭你们应邀前来，还把这里搞得一团乱？
t_en: Let me ask you a question: Have you ever wondered why The Family allowed the Watchmaker to send out such a ridiculous message to the outside world, inviting you here and causing chaos?

t_ch: 你们想借这个机会让「钟表匠」露出马脚？
t_en: So, you want to seize this opportunity to expose the Watchmaker?

t_ch: 现在你能理解橡木家系为何授权无名客协助调查，却又处处对你们有所隐瞒了吧。
t_en: Well, now you understand why the Oak Family authorized the Nameless to assist in the investigation but kept you in the dark, right?

t_ch: …因为「钟表匠」根本不是什么梦想之地的传奇，而是<color=#dbc291ff>匹诺康尼分家史上最不可告人的污点</color>，他就是一切梦境异变的始作俑者。
t_en: ...Because the Watchmaker is not the legend of the Land of the Dreams at all. He's <color=#dbc291ff>the most shameful stain in the history of Penacony</color>, and he's the root cause of all the anomalies in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 这和米哈伊尔又有什么关系？
t_en: But what does this have to do with Mikhail?

t_ch: 还没反应过来吗？我的意思是……
t_en: You don't get it? Well, I mean...

t_ch: …<color=#dbc291ff>米哈伊尔，家族的背叛者——他就是大名鼎鼎的「钟表匠」</color>啊。
t_en: ...<color=#dbc291ff>Mikhail, the betrayer of The Family... He's the Watchmaker</color>.

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...

t_ch: 喏——「克劳克影视乐园」，匹诺康尼最受欢迎的影视娱乐中心。
t_en: Here we are, Clock Studios Theme Park, the most popular entertainment center in Penacony.

t_ch: 不是要讲「钟表匠」的事吗？我还以为会去资料室之类的地方，怎么是这里……
t_en: Wait, aren't we supposed to be discussing "The Watchmaker"? I would have expected you to take us to maybe a library or an archive room of sorts, but an amusement park...?

t_ch: 一座城市的文化就是历史最真实的注脚。在你眼中这是玩乐的地方，但在我眼中，它是一座监牢，用来监禁这颗星球的过往。
t_en: The culture of a city reflects its history in the most authentic way. To you, it's a fun place, but to me, it's a prison for the planet's past.

t_ch: 你们肯定知道匹诺康尼曾经是公司的监狱星，对吧？犯人们被押送来这里，帮流光忆庭打捞大孔洞里泄漏的忆质……
t_en: You know that Penacony used to be the IPC's prison planet, right? All the prisoners were brought here, helping the Garden of Recollection salvage the leaking memoria from the Macro Void...

t_ch: 监狱长期暴露在高浓度的忆质中，产生了一种特殊的现象。无数个体的梦境交错重叠，人们开始在梦中相会，过上恍如现实的生活。
t_en: The prolonged exposure to high concentrations of memoria caused a unique phenomenon. The dreams of countless prisoners intersected and overlapped, and people started meeting each other in their dreams, living lives that were almost identical to reality.

t_ch: 但凡事总有代价，美梦也不例外。最后，梦境也无法消解人们现实中的苦难，在一位囚犯的带领下，边陲监狱砸碎了公司的镣铐，开始为自由而战……
t_en: But everything has a price, and sweet dreams are no exception. In the end, the dream world was unable to alleviate the suffering of prisoners in reality. One of the prisoners broke free from the IPC's shackles and fought for freedom...

t_ch: …他就是{RUBY_B#哈努努}「哈努兄弟」{RUBY_E#}。梦境小镇的老大哥，和平的建立者，弱者永远的伙伴。
t_en: ...He is {RUBY_B#Hanunue}Hanu{RUBY_E#}, the great leader of Dreamville, the great peacemaker, and the faithful companion of the underdogs.

t_ch: 哈努兄弟的原型就是哈努努？
t_en: So, the character "Hanu" is based on a real-life person?

t_ch: 原来《钟表小子》是纪录片…
t_en: So that Clockie cartoon is actually a documentary...

t_ch: 历史向来是由胜者书写，但抛开艺术加工的部分，《钟表小子》是以匹诺康尼历史为背景创作的动画，这是不争的事实。
t_en: History is always written by the winners. However, it's undeniable that Clockie is an animation that draws from Penacony's actual history.

t_ch: 这些角色不仅生活在美梦小镇中，也生活在遥远的过去。明白了这点…也就明白了我们为什么要来这里。
t_en: These characters not only exist in Dreamville but also in the distant past. Once you realize this... you'll understand why we're here.

t_ch: 嚯…阵仗够大啊。追捕嫌犯时都没见有这么卖力。
t_en: Heh... so many of them... I've never seen anything like this, even when they're tracking down suspects.

t_ch: 能让他们放行吗？
t_en: Can you convince them to let us in?

t_ch: 不需要，里头人多口杂，咱们站外边说就行。
t_en: We don't need to go in — we don't want to draw any unwanted attention inside. We can just talk here.

t_ch: 光从外面看也很震撼。
t_en: It's pretty impressive even from out here.

t_ch: 毕竟是家族的手笔。除了信仰「存护」的那帮人，就属他们最擅长建造奇观。
t_en: Well, it's a masterpiece from The Family after all. Besides the followers of the Preservation, The Family members are the best at creating mind-blowing marvels.

t_ch: 找个安静的角落继续吧。
t_en: Let's find a quiet spot and continue our conversation.

t_ch: 就这里吧，视野不错。正好可以望见他……
t_en: The view here is great, right? We can see everything from here...

t_ch: …「钟表小子」。
t_en: ...Including Clockie.

t_ch: 如果这些动画角色在现实中都有迹可循，那钟表小子对应的，毫无疑问就是「钟表匠」了。
t_en: If all the characters in the animation are based on characters in reality, then Clockie's counterpart is definitely the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 他是哈努兄弟的伙伴，是美梦小镇最初的几名成员之一，这是否可以理解为历史上的「钟表匠」也亲身参与了那场战争，并且站在阿斯德纳这边？
t_en: In the animation, he's Hanu's partner and one of the founders of Dreamville. Does that mean the Watchmaker was personally involved in that war and sided with Asdana?

t_ch: 那是场声势浩大的独立战争，假面愚者、无名客、虚构史学家、悲悼伶人、厄兆先锋…哈努努在一众同伴和天外来客的帮助下平定了战乱。
t_en: It was a monumental war for freedom. Hanunue fought alongside a motley crew of Masked Fools, Nameless, History Fictionologists, Mourning Actors, Omen Vanguards... even visitors from beyond the sky. In the end, they emerged victorious.

t_ch: 自然，那其中也有日后的「钟表匠」。
t_en: Among them was the person who would eventually be known as the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 可这么一算…「钟表匠」岂不是活了好几百年？
t_en: But if you do the math... doesn't that mean the Watchmaker was around for several centuries?

t_ch: 不知道。我认识米哈伊尔时，他就已经是「钟表匠」了，也可能是继承的名号。
t_en: I'm not sure, but Mikhail was already the Watchmaker when I met him, so maybe he inherited the title.

t_ch: …治安官先生，你多大了？
t_en: ...How old are you now, Mr. Officer?

t_ch: …十三岁。
t_en: ...I'm thirteen.

t_ch: 怎么看都不可能吧！
t_en: No way! Not even close!

t_ch: 哈努努解放了边陲监狱，但没来得及看见和平就走了。贫瘠的资源，虎视眈眈的外部，离心离德的各大监区…阿斯德纳的未来依旧岌岌可危。
t_en: Hanunue freed the frontier prison, but peace still eluded him. With limited resources, threats from the outside world, and internal conflicts between major prison districts, the future of Asdana was uncertain.

t_ch: 直到历史上的「钟表匠」向家族抛出橄榄枝，试图将这座监狱打造成觥筹交错的盛会之星…「匹诺康尼」才终于获得它如今的名字，走上面向群星的舞台。
t_en: It wasn't until the Watchmaker approached The Family with the idea of turning the prison into the Planet of Festivities... that Penacony finally gained its name and glory.

t_ch: 所以他才被称作「匹诺康尼之父」。
t_en: Thus he became known as the Father of Penacony.

t_ch: 可是，你前面明明说「钟表匠」是家族的背叛者？你还说自己是他的同伴，所以你也……
t_en: But didn't you say the Watchmaker betrayed The Family? And you said you were his companion, so that means you...

t_ch: 我不是他的同伴，是他众多「孩子」的一员。
t_en: No, I'm not his "companion," but rather one of his many "children."

t_ch: 但我确实是叛徒，不是背叛家族，而是背叛了…米哈伊尔。
t_en: But I am indeed a traitor. Not to The Family, but to... Mikhail.

t_ch: …你做了什么？
t_en: ...What did you do?

t_ch: 我什么都没做…这就是最大的背叛。
t_en: I did nothing... and that's the worst betrayal of all.

t_ch: 就像你们一样，我也曾拥有亲密无间的伙伴。我们为匹诺康尼呕心沥血，可*橡木家系*…却陷我们于不义。
t_en: Just like you, I had close companions. We dedicated ourselves to Penacony, but the Oak Family... they set us up.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔老了，不能再保护他的孩子。我们离开家族，自寻出路，就成了「同谐」的叛徒…尽管真正的背叛者另有其人。
t_en: Mikhail was too old to protect his children anymore, so we left The Family to find our own path. We were branded traitors of the Harmony, even though the true traitors were someone else.

t_ch: 他们对外依旧称赞「钟表匠」的美名，暗地里却悄悄地将他钉上耻辱柱。
t_en: While they continue to praise the Watchmaker's name to the world, behind closed doors they condemn him on a pillar of shame.

t_ch: 即便如此，我们还是希望为他正名。只要能把真正的叛徒，这一切的幕后主使揪出，匹诺康尼的「同谐」便能重回正轨……
t_en: Nevertheless, we wanted to clear his name. We intended to find the real traitor, the one responsible for all this, and restore Harmony to Penacony...

t_ch: …但我们输了。漫长的时间过去，梦想之地受到的影响已经太深，在没有尽头的穷追猛打下，我放弃了…就像一条丧家犬。
t_en: ...But we failed. Too much time had passed, and the Land of the Dreams had become deeply corrupted. After countless fruitless pursuits, I gave up... like a lost dog.

t_ch: 家族重新接纳了我，给了我治安官的工作，表面是宽恕，实际是惩罚。自此，我和伙伴…和我的过去彻底断了联系。而米哈伊尔……
t_en: The Family accepted me and made me an officer, supposedly as a form of forgiveness, but it was actually a punishment. Since then, I've been completely cut off from my partners... and my past. As for Mikhail...

t_ch: …我听说他死在了无人在意的角落里，一个没有人能发现的地方。我明白，从这一刻起，曾经的匹诺康尼再也回不来了。
t_en: ...I heard he died in obscurity, in a place where no one could find him. That's when I realized that the Penacony I once knew would never return.

t_ch: 我们对这个故事深表遗憾…但事情没有这么简单，对吧？
t_en: We're truly sorry for what happened... but this is not the end of the story, right?

t_ch: 哼…显然，有人继承了「钟表匠」之名，在暗地里持续进行反抗家族的活动，直至现在。
t_en: Hmm... Apparently, someone has inherited the title of the Watchmaker and has been secretly working against The Family all this time.

t_ch: 这人是谁？
t_en: Who could that person be?

t_ch: 「钟表匠」是个组织？
t_en: Is the Watchmaker an organization?

t_ch: 也可以这么理解。不过继承了「钟表匠」名号的应该只有一人。
t_en: Well, that's one way to look at it. However, only one member has truly inherited the Watchmaker's title.

t_ch: 可惜啊，这么多年过去，我还是不知道那人是谁，甚至不知道那到底是个人…还是米哈伊尔的幽灵在梦中游荡。
t_en: Unfortunately, after all these years, I have no idea who that person is, or if they're even real... or just Mikhail's lost soul haunting the dreams.

t_ch: 所以明白了么？为什么我肯和你们说这么多…因为那姑娘的死一定和「钟表匠的遗产」有关。而在重重迷雾的尽头……
t_en: So, do you understand why I'm spilling all this info...? Because I believe the girl's death must be connected to the Watchmaker's Legacy, and at the end of all these mysteries...

t_ch: …你我都能得到想要的答案。
t_en: ...We will find the answers we are seeking.

t_ch: 如果那真是米哈伊尔的幽灵作祟，我还挺想见见他的。看不起我的人可以绕酒店三圈，但愿意正眼瞧我的人…哎，死一个就少一个了。
t_en: If it really is Mikhail's ghost, I want to meet him, if only for the last time. Those who despise me could form a line from here all the way to the entrance of the hotel, but those willing to look me in the eye and hear me out... Let's just say, there won't be many.

t_ch: 我知道的都告诉你们了，权当各位愿意瞧我这条老狗一眼的谢礼。
t_en: I've told you all I know as a sign of gratitude. Thank you for listening to this old dog — bark and all.

t_ch: …嗯？影城那边好像有点事…先失陪了，祝你们好运。
t_en: ...Hmm? Something just happened at the theme park... Now, if you'll excuse me. Good luck to all of you.

t_ch: 真讽刺啊，如今被匹诺康尼拒之门外的这些偷渡客，和几个琥珀纪前被奉为拓荒者的逐梦客…又有什么差别呢？
t_en: How ironic. What's so different between the stowaways rejected by Penacony today and the dream seekers once hailed as pioneers several Amber Eras ago?

t_ch: 加拉赫先生…果然是很有故事的人啊。
t_en: Gallagher... does have a troubled past, it seems.

t_ch: 虽然流萤小姐的去向依旧成谜，但他的分享解释了不少疑点：「钟表匠」的真实身份，与家族的关系，以及隐藏在美梦和死亡背后的势力斗争。
t_en: While Firefly's whereabouts remain a mystery, his stories shed light on our suspicions about the true identity of the Watchmaker, his connection to The Family, and the power struggles hidden behind sweet dreams and death.

t_ch: 还有…家族并非万众一心。
t_en: And that... The Family isn't all "united as one".

t_ch: 没错，加拉赫先生提到真正的背叛者另有其人…并且很可能潜伏在橡木家系中。
t_en: Exactly. Gallagher suggests that the real traitor is someone else... probably within the Oak Family.

t_ch: 还有…「死亡」和钟表匠有关。
t_en: And that... "Death" is related to the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 这也和之前的信息对得上，流萤小姐正是为了那份遗产才会被卷入这场惨剧…我们也更能确信，砂金对黄泉的指控并无根据。
t_en: That lines up with what we've gathered so far. Firefly got involved in this mess because of the Legacy... And now we're sure that Aventurine's accusations against Acheron are baseless.

t_ch: 还有…钟表小子是钟表匠。
t_en: And that... Clockie is based on the Watchmaker.

t_ch: …你就这么喜欢钟表小子嘛！那只是个动画形象啦，又不是本人！
t_en: ...You're really into Clockie, huh? He's just a fictional character, not a real person!

t_ch: 这么说来，那位只有{NICKNAME}才能看见的钟表小子也令人在意…可惜那之后就没再遇见他了。
t_en: Speaking of which, that Clockie who only reveals himself to {NICKNAME} is quite intriguing... It's a shame we've never met him since then.

t_ch: 差不多就这些了。
t_en: That pretty much sums it up.

t_ch: 此行验证了不少先前的猜想，值得我们再仔细消化下。{NICKNAME}，给瓦尔特发条消息，问问他那边情况如何吧。
t_en: Now that we've confirmed a lot of our suspicions, let's take a moment to think about the clues we have. {NICKNAME}, send a message to Welt and see how things are going on his end.

t_ch: 收获不少啊！不知道这些够不够……
t_en: That's quite a stash! Not sure if it's enough...

t_ch: ——让开！我要找舒翁！
t_en: Stay out of my way! I'm looking for Siobhan!

t_ch: 咦，什么动静？
t_en: Huh, what's all the commotion about?

t_ch: 这附近，好多猎犬家系的人啊……
t_en: There are so many members of the Bloodhound Family around here...

t_ch: 刚收到了封锁令，说是那位星期日亲自下达的，天知道要干什么。
t_en: They just received a lockdown order, supposedly from Sunday himself. Who knows what it's for.

t_ch: 治安官与无人问津的酒吧…有意思。
t_en: A security officer and a derelict lounge... Interesting.

t_ch: …同伴在担心你吗？
t_en: ...Are your companions worried about you?

t_ch: 只是正常的联络。他们那边似乎有些发现，我们快去快回吧。
t_en: They're just checking up on me. Let's get in and get out — it seems they've made some progress.

t_ch: …再向前走，就要进入朝露公馆深处了。
t_en: ...Looks like we're about to enter the depths of Dewlight Pavilion.

t_ch: 一路上都很顺利…顺利过头了，很难想象这是防守严密的家族驻地。
t_en: It's been a smooth ride... almost too smooth for a heavily guarded mansion.

t_ch: 先找找有没有可以通报的人吧。
t_en: Let's see if there's anyone waiting to greet us.

t_ch: 不对劲，如此宏伟的宅邸，竟然连个接待客人的管家都没有…是因为突发事件造成的空窗？
t_en: Something feels off... A grand mansion like this and not a butler or servant in sight... Could it be due to the disruption caused by the emergency?

t_ch: 这扇门开着，看来只能一探究竟了。谨慎前进吧。
t_en: ...This door is open — looks like we'll have to investigate ourselves. Let's proceed with caution.

t_ch: 稍等。
t_en: Just one moment.

t_ch: 黄泉微微拔刀。顷刻间，她的气息变得微不可知。
t_en: Acheron draws her blade slightly. Within a moment, her breath became imperceptible.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#气息隐匿}「白」{RUBY_E#}…我微微消除了自己周身的气息，这样更不容易被注意到。星穹列车有家族授意还能解释，但我出现在这…并不合理。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Bated Breath}"White"{RUBY_E#}... I've made myself less noticeable. The Crew can explain their presence as authorized by The Family, but I can't come up with any excuses for being here.

t_ch: 原来如此，很有趣的技艺。
t_en: I see. What an interesting technique.

t_ch: 这里的脚印与别处不同…共有两道。不久前可能有外来者从这里走过。
t_en: The footprints here are different from the rest... there are two sets of them. Looks like outsiders might have passed through here not long ago.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生能分辨出脚印的主人是谁吗？
t_en: Can you identify the people who left these footprints?

t_ch: 嗯…这花纹样式独特，甚是张扬。从尺码来看，应该是男款。
t_en: Well... there's a unique pattern here — flamboyant even. And judging by the size, I'd say these were men's shoes.

t_ch: 我猜…八成是那位星际和平公司的使节，砂金先生。
t_en: If I'm right... it could be the IPC ambassador, Aventurine.

t_ch: 砂金…那另一位呢？
t_en: Aventurine... What about the other set?

t_ch: 两道足迹并非一前一后。从几乎并行的状态来看，同行者应该不是他的下属……
t_en: It looks like they were walking side by side as opposed to one behind the other — so the second individual is likely equal in status to Aventurine.

t_ch: …公司渴望收回匹诺康尼。他们出现在这里，倒也在意料之中。
t_en: ...The IPC is eager to reclaim Penacony, so their presence here is not unexpected.

t_ch: 尽管没有人的气息…但他们布置了不少迷因在这巡逻啊。
t_en: While there aren't any people in this mansion... they've set up quite a few memetic guards to patrol this place.

t_ch: 它身上好像掉了什么东西。
t_en: Look, it dropped something.

t_ch: 这是…便条？似乎是管家给其他仆从留下的指示。
t_en: A... note? Looks like instructions from the butler for the other servants.

t_ch: 看来朝露公馆的工作人员被分配了其他任务…并且这事早在知更鸟死前就开始了。
t_en: Seems like the mansion's entire workforce were assigned other tasks... before Robin's death.

t_ch: …也许是需要大量人力的工程，比如「谐乐大典」。
t_en: ...It must have been a big project to require that much manpower... the "Charmony Festival," perhaps.

t_ch: 但按理来说…无论有什么重大要务，家族禁地总该留些人在后方待命吧？
t_en: But no matter what their main priorities are, there should always be someone left at the mansion, right?

t_ch: 你是说…是有人故意支开了他们？
t_en: So, you're saying... someone deliberately cleared the place out?

t_ch: 嗯，但不知道是为了什么。
t_en: Yeah, but I don't know why.

t_ch: …这里依然没有人。
t_en: ...No one here either.

t_ch: 既然没人招待，我们就自便吧。请尽量靠近我些，这样「白」也能覆盖到你。
t_en: Since no one's around to entertain us, let's make ourselves at home. Stay close to me so that my "White" can cover you, too.

t_ch: 列车组刚刚抵达匹诺康尼不久，星期日先生就带着知更鸟小姐前来接待了我们。那时我便觉得她的声音有一丝古怪…现在看来，确有其事。
t_en: As soon as I and the rest of the Crew arrived in Penacony, Mr. Sunday and Robin showed up to greet us. I remember hearing something unusual in her voice... and now it seems I was right.

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐认为这是因为「同谐」受到了某种干扰。但据我所知，能够干涉命途伟力的存在少之又少。
t_en: Robin believed it was because the Harmony had been tampered with somehow, but as far as I know, there aren't many entities capable of interfering with the power of Paths.

t_ch: 黄泉小姐的意思是……
t_en: Meaning...

t_ch: 如果家族中真的存在叛徒…那人必定要身居高位，或拥有深不可测的实力。
t_en: If there really is a traitor within The Family... that person must hold a high position or possess unimaginable strength.

t_ch: 这也解释了星期日先生迟迟没能抓住叛徒的原因。
t_en: That would explain why Mr. Sunday has been having such difficulty in catching the traitor.

t_ch: 知更鸟、流萤…还有其他死者的信息…看不出其中有何共性。
t_en: The information about Robin, Firefly... and the other victims... I don't see any commonalities among them.

t_ch: 看来坊间传闻不错——「死亡」确实是在随机挑选受害者。并且从星期日的批注内容来看…他对「死亡」并不陌生。
t_en: Looks like the rumors were right — "Death" does seem to be targeting random victims. And based on Sunday's notes... he's no stranger to "Death."

t_ch: 他只是惊讶于它的再次出现。
t_en: He's just surprised that it has resurfaced.

t_ch: 星期日先生对嫌疑人的研究非常深入——这名叛徒一定困扰家族已久了。
t_en: Mr. Sunday has done some serious research on his suspects... This traitor must have been causing trouble for The Family for a long time.

t_ch: 看起来都是家族的内部人员啊…但我没见过其中任何一人。
t_en: They all seem to be insiders... but I haven't met any of them.

t_ch: 咦？这些特征……
t_en: Huh? Wait, these characteristics...

t_ch: 黄泉小姐想到了什么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 没什么，应该是我多虑了…如果这名叛徒真的存在，会不会就是他害死了流萤和知更鸟小姐？
t_en: No, nothing... Maybe I'm just overthinking things... However, if this traitor really exists, could they be responsible for Firefly and Robin's deaths?

t_ch: 或许这就是星期日如此重视这件事的原因。
t_en: Perhaps that's why Sunday is taking this matter so seriously.

t_ch: 这张光锥…被严格保管起来了。他应该很在乎这份记忆。
t_en: This Light Cone... is securely guarded. It must hold some important memories.

t_ch: 知更鸟的采访中曾提及，即便她登上过如此多华丽的舞台，她最珍视的演出还是小时候和哥哥过家家时一起办的演唱会。
t_en: According to Robin's interview, despite having performed on so many grand stages, her favorite performance was a pretend show she put on with her brother when they were just kids.

t_ch: 不知他们现在关系如何。
t_en: I wonder how their relationship is now.

t_ch: 成长的过程中总会获得许多，但也会失去许多。
t_en: Growing up brings gains, but also losses.

t_ch: 是啊，时光会抚平一切。年少的美梦…也终会醒来。
t_en: Yeah, time has a way of smoothing things out. The beautiful dreams of youth... will eventually fade away.

t_ch: 看来匹诺康尼的「梦主」和这位老奥帝先生都对星期日最近的表现不太满意。
t_en: It seems neither the Dreammaster of Penacony nor this Old Oti is happy with Sunday's recent performance.

t_ch: 他们好像不是那么在意「死亡」…反而更在意谐乐大典和「钟表匠」。
t_en: They don't seem to care much about "Death"... Instead, they're more concerned about the Charmony Festival and the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 也许其他家主并不觉得「死亡」是什么大问题…家族内部也是互相掣肘，矛盾重重啊。
t_en: Maybe the other family heads don't think "Death" is a big deal... One thing's for sure — there's a lot of internal conflict within The Family, and everybody has their own agenda.

t_ch: 就到这里吧…似乎没什么值得注意的了。
t_en: That's all for now... Nothing more noteworthy.

t_ch: 踏入大门前，我设想过种种与家族打交道的场面，可没想到迎接我们的只有一座空宅……
t_en: Before coming here, I had all sorts of scenarios in my head about dealing with The Family... I did not expect an empty mansion...

t_ch: …小心，有人来了。
t_en: ...Watch out. Someone's approaching.

t_ch: 未经允许，擅闯禁地，这恐怕不是作客之道吧？瓦尔特先生，还有……
t_en: I don't think trespassing on forbidden areas is the way to be a guest, Mr. Yang, and...

t_ch: …巡海游侠，黄泉女士？
t_en: ...Acheron, the Galaxy Ranger?

t_ch: 抱歉，星期日先生。我们没找到任何可以通报的人员，才擅自进入贵府，还请您多加原谅。
t_en: Our apologies, Mr. Sunday. Nobody came to greet us, so we entered without permission. I hope you can forgive us.

t_ch: 可即便无人接待，二位也应静坐等候主人到来，不是么？
t_en: But even if there's no one to greet you, you should wait for the host, don't you agree?

t_ch: 这位巡海游侠暂且不提…就我所知，星穹列车已经在正式场合接受了家族的委托，怕是没必要再特地大驾光临了。
t_en: Even without the famous Galaxy Ranger... as far as I know, the Crew has officially accepted The Family's commission, so coming here will be unnecessary for you.

t_ch: 我们此行前来，正是为了同星期日先生了解案情，以免调查过程中出现闪失。
t_en: On the contrary, that's exactly why we're here. To ask you about the case and gather more information — we don't want any loose ends.

t_ch: …罢了。既然二位带着善意前来，我也没有再下逐客令的理由。
t_en: ...Well, since you've come with goodwill, I have no reason to show you the door.

t_ch: <size=-2>放心…他没发现我们翻阅了那些文件。</size>
t_en: <size=-2>Rest assured... He hasn't figured out that we went through those documents.</size>

t_ch: 真相虽未水落石出，但我已经离它不远。我向各位保证，叛徒很快就会付出代价。
t_en: While the truth remains a mystery, I'm getting close to it. I assure you that the traitor will soon pay the price.

t_ch: 愿公义早日得以彰显，但我个人还有一点疑问想请教星期日先生：家族究竟是如何断定凶手潜藏在内部的？
t_en: Let's hope justice will prevail soon. I have a question for you, if you don't mind: How did The Family come to the conclusion that the murderer was within The Family?

t_ch: 恕我直言，有人试图在谐乐大典召开前夕引发混乱，这恰恰符合公司的利益…家族应当有理由怀疑是公司在从中作梗。
t_en: With all due respect, it's in the IPC's interest to wreak havoc before the Charmony Festival... and The Family has every reason to suspect the IPC's involvement.

t_ch: 其他家主也提出了和您一样的疑虑。但在我看来，真正的凶手绝不会像那位使节一样招摇过市…更何况我早已亲手为他套上枷锁。
t_en: Well, other family heads share the same suspicions as you, but in my opinion, the true murderer would never have drawn as much attention as that ambassador did... Not to mention I personally shackled him a while ago.

t_ch: 不过，我反而要将瓦尔特先生的疑虑提示给二位。在我看来，更应当对砂金保持警惕的是你们…恶人固然无法撼动高墙，但却能将尖刀刺进义人的心脏。
t_en: However, I'll give you a suggestion regarding your suspicions, Mr. Yang. You should be more cautious of Aventurine... While the wicked can't break through high walls, they can plunge their evil dagger into the heart of the righteous.

t_ch: 他是商人，不是慈善家，可眼下他正在街道上四处散财，又独自去往克劳克影视乐园的方向，不知打的是什么算盘……
t_en: He's a businessman, not some philanthropist. But right now, he's out there handing out his wealth on the streets, and he went to the Clock Studios Theme Park all by himself. Who knows what kind of scheme he's cooking up...?

t_ch: 家族依旧承诺会保护来宾周全，但各位也不妨留个心眼。
t_en: While The Family is dedicated to keeping our guests safe, it might be wise for you to stay alert.

t_ch: 以免…不测之忧。
t_en: You never know... what unexpected troubles could arise.

t_ch: 那沙盘里的模型…应该是黄金的时刻。
t_en: The model in the sandpit... it's the Golden Hour, isn't it?

t_ch: 也许家主们就是用这座模型来商议要事的。
t_en: Maybe the heads of The Family use that model for discussing important matters.

t_ch: 「…据庇尔波因特热线消息，骇人听闻的『艾吉哈佐砂金案』获得重大突破，犯罪嫌疑人现已落网——」
t_en: "...According to a Pier Point hotline tip, there was a major breakthrough in the shocking Egyhazo Aventurine case. The suspect has been arrested..."

t_ch: 「该诈骗案牵连星际和平公司与博识学会多个部门，导致大量人力物力资源浪费，令公司蒙受巨额损失——」
t_en: "This fraud case has been linked to many departments within the Interastral Peace Corporation and the Intelligentsia Guild, causing a large drain in manpower and resources, resulting in the IPC taking a massive loss..."

t_ch: 「本案嫌疑人来自茨冈尼亚-Ⅳ，是『第二次卡提卡-埃维金灭绝案件』的幸存者之一，且并未持有星际难民旅行证——」
t_en: "The case's main suspect originates from Sigonia-IV, and is one of the survivors of the Second Katica-Avgin Extinction Event, who does not carry an Interstellar Refugee Travel Permit..."

t_ch: 「在战略投资部主管『钻石』的示意下，公司基于《宪章》精神对其妥善安置，并将持续开展调查工作，进一步确认嫌疑人的犯罪动机……」
t_en: "As per Strategic Investment Department head Diamond's sentiments, the IPC has appropriately relocated the suspect in the spirit of the Charter, and will continue to conduct further investigations as to the motive of the suspect..."

t_ch: 真是双漂亮的眼睛。告诉我，它们会在夜里发光吗？
t_en: What pretty eyes. Tell me, do they shine in the dark?

t_ch: 如果可以，我一定会把它们卖掉的。
t_en: Well if they did, I'd sell them in a heartbeat.

t_ch: 你不知道有多少人盼着你永远闭上眼睛。身为奴隶，你不该反抗主人的…可你却把那个男人干掉了。
t_en: You don't know how many people long for your eyes to be closed forever. As a servant, you should not resist your master... Yet you went and killed that man anyway.

t_ch: 没有律师敢为你辩护，或许你该试着替自己争取一下无罪声明？
t_en: No lawyer has the audacity to defend you, perhaps you ought to represent yourself?

t_ch: 这不难，但没有意义。
t_en: Not difficult. But definitely pointless.

t_ch: 对口才很自信嘛。在欺骗博识学会时，你也是这么想的？
t_en: You're pretty confident on your eloquence. Did you also think that when you lied to the Intelligentsia Guild?

t_ch: 求仁得仁罢了。你们想要完美的筑材，我只是给了一种可能性，一场小小的赌局。
t_en: Ask and you shall receive. You wanted the perfect construction material — all I did was offer a possibility. It was just a small wager.

t_ch: 如果运气好，公司能从艾吉哈佐的黄沙里淘出任何东西，甚至{RUBY_B#塔伊兹育罗斯}「沙王」{RUBY_E#}的残骸。可惜，你们运气不行。
t_en: If your luck holds out, the IPC will dig something up from the golden sands of Egyhazo, maybe even the {RUBY_B#Tayzzyronth}Sand King's{RUBY_E#} remains. Pity, your luck has run out.

t_ch: 这点我不否认。但我好奇的是，为何一场如此兴师动众的骗局，到头来却没有一个人从中获利——包括犯人自己？
t_en: I'll admit that. What I'm more curious about though, is why such a grand scheme failed to benefit anyone in the end, including the perpetrator himself?

t_ch: 女士，我已得到了想要的——被带到你的面前，开启下一场豪赌。
t_en: Madam, I already have what I want — to be brought before you, for the next high-stakes gamble.

t_ch: 那就来谈谈这第二场豪赌吧。说说看，这回你打算押什么？
t_en: Then let's talk about the second gamble. Tell me, what are you prepared to wager this time?

t_ch: 押我的命。我赌你不会把我送上刑场。
t_en: My life. I bet you won't send me to the gallows.

t_ch: 嗯…那你想得到什么？
t_en: Hmm... What do you want then?

t_ch: 我要你们的{RUBY_B#酋长}拉拿{RUBY_E#}来见我。我有话要说。
t_en: I want your {RUBY_B#Chief}Lanor{RUBY_E#} to meet with me. I have something to say.

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: And then what?

t_ch: 我要钱。
t_en: I want cash.

t_ch: 不会这么简单吧？
t_en: It can't be that simple, can it?

t_ch: 就这么简单，三十枚{RUBY_B#赤铜币}塔安巴{RUBY_E#}，我半条命的价格，不多不少。
t_en: It is that simple. Thirty {RUBY_B#Red Copper Coins}Tanbas{RUBY_E#} — the remainder of my... market value. Thirty Tanbas, no more, no less.

t_ch: 只要有了这些钱，我就能爬到比你更高的位置，手握比你更多的财富…我赌你不敢给我，所以，叫他过来吧。
t_en: With this money, I'll climb to even greater heights than you, grasp even more riches than you... I wager you won't give me this chance... which is why you should call him here.

t_ch: 有趣。
t_en: Interesting.

t_ch: 可惜「钻石」不会见你，谁也见不到他。所以此刻，我就是「钻石」的代理人，替他做出决定。
t_en: A pity Diamond won't see you. No one gets to see him. From here on out, I am Diamond's representative, and I will decide on his behalf.

t_ch: 你错了。三十枚塔安巴，我会给你，并且远比这更多。财富、地位、权力…公司会给你想要和不想要的一切。
t_en: You're wrong. Thirty Tanbas... I'll give you that, and much more than that. Wealth, status, power... the IPC will give you whatever you want, even what you don't want.

t_ch: 「卡卡瓦夏」…是个好名字，可惜注定要被埋进土里。但「你」值得活下来，为我们创造更多财富。
t_en: Kakavasha... A good name, but unfortunately destined to be buried in the dirt. You though, you deserve to live, to create even more wealth for us.

t_ch: 去吧，给自己挑身喜欢的衣服，再选个中意的身份…然后活用它们，孩子。
t_en: Go, pick the clothes you like, then choose your desired identity... and then use them well, child.

t_ch: 愿你的诡计永不败露。
t_en: May your schemes be forever concealed.

t_ch: 生命就像一场漫长的投资，选择正确的人，做出正确的事，抵达正确的结果，向世界展示自己的价值。
t_en: Life is like a long-term investment. Those who choose correctly, do the correct things, reach the correct outcomes, show the world their value.

t_ch: 人不可能一辈子只做正确的决定，但好运总是站在我这边。我从未输过。
t_en: People can't always make the right choices in their lives, but luck has always been on my side. I've never lost.

t_ch: 是因为母神在保佑我吗？既然如此，此刻她也一定注视着我吧。我定然能获得成功。
t_en: Is it because Gaiathra blesses me? If that's the case, she must also be looking upon me right now. My success is inevitable.

t_ch: …可是，然后呢？
t_en: ...But, what then?

t_ch: 倘若我成功度过这道难关，接下来又是什么？在一场盛大的赌局后等待着我的…是另一场更盛大的赌局吗？
t_en: Even if I overcome this difficult trial, what would come next? What awaits me after this glorious gamble... an even more glorious one?

t_ch: 是在一次又一次成功后，带着不可胜数的筹码满载而归，还是在一次失败后……
t_en: Will I return triumphant with unending riches after countless successes, or will I encounter failure...

t_ch: …便一去不回？
t_en: ...never to return?

t_ch: 难道你心里没点数吗……
t_en: I'm pretty sure you already have some ideas...

t_ch: …卑贱的赌徒？
t_en: ...Lowly gambler?

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: Wh—What?

t_ch: 我是在做梦，还是彻底疯了？
t_en: Am I dreaming, or have I gone completely insane?

t_ch: 也许两者都是。
t_en: Perhaps both.

t_ch: 这就把我忘了？你被伊伊玛尼喀的军阀绑在电刑椅上的时候，是谁给你出的主意？
t_en: Forgotten me already? When you were strapped to that electric chair by Iymanikan warlords, who was it that gave you the idea?

t_ch: 行了…我可能疯，但不傻。从我脑袋里滚出去，「同谐」的新生儿。
t_en: Fine... I may be crazy, but I'm not stupid. Get out of my head, newborn of the Harmony.

t_ch: 呵，「同谐」？别傻了，我们又不是第一次见面，不用这么见外吧？
t_en: Heh, the Harmony? Don't play the fool — it's not the first time we've met. No need to be so polite.

t_ch: 我就是你，甚至比你更了解「自己」，更知道你到底想要什么。你快死了，死到临头还想拉几个倒霉蛋一起上路…所以你才会来这儿，不是么？
t_en: I'm you, and perhaps even more aware of yourself than you, of what exactly you want. You're dying, and you still want to drag a bunch of unfortunate fools with you through death's door... That's why you're here, isn't it?

t_ch: …伟大的揭幕表演，你真觉得自己能做到吗？
t_en: ...A grand unveiling. You really think you can pull it off?

t_ch: 有何不可？
t_en: Why not?

t_ch: 也许你骗得了所有人，但唯独骗不了你自己。
t_en: Well you may have fooled everyone, but you can't fool yourself.

t_ch: 我可以证明给你看——在你彻底消失前，我会陪你最后走一段路……
t_en: I can show you — before you're entirely gone, I'll be with you for the last stretch of your road...

t_ch: 咱们就在路上好好说道说道。
t_en: Let's have a heart-to-heart while we walk.

t_ch: …该死的，你到底是什么鬼东西？
t_en: ...What exactly are you?

t_ch: 这世上的大部分人，终其一生只为抵达一种结果…而我就是那个结果。
t_en: Most people in this world spend their entire lives just to reach one outcome... and I am that outcome.

t_ch: 「卡卡瓦夏」…我是你的未来。
t_en: Kakavasha... I am your future.

t_ch: 记得「黄金的时刻」是不允许未成年人进入的…喂，你怎么一个人在这？
t_en: I thought minors weren't allowed in Golden Hour... Hey kid, are you okay? Are you lost—
— Ugh!

t_ch: 怎么了，先生…你看起来不太舒服？
t_en: What's wrong, Mister... You don't look well.

t_ch: 你的…眼睛？
t_en: Your... eyes?

t_ch: …这不可能。你是什么人？
t_en: ...Impossible. Who are you?

t_ch: 它们很漂亮，对吧？姐姐说，那是{RUBY_B#地母神}「芬戈妈妈」{RUBY_E#}的礼物。彩色的眸子能给人带来好运。
t_en: They're pretty, aren't they? Sis said they're a gift from {RUBY_B#Gaiathra}Mama Fenge{RUBY_E#}. Colorful eyes are said to bring good luck.

t_ch: 啊，先生…你也有双漂亮的眼睛。真好看！
t_en: Ah, Mister... You have pretty eyes too. Beautiful!

t_ch: 你…就一个人吗？你的父母呢？
t_en: Are... you alone? Where are your parents?

t_ch: 他们都在这座游乐园里，爸爸妈妈先进去了。我正要去找他们。
t_en: They're in that amusement park. Papa and Mama went in first. I'm just about to go look for them.

t_ch: 所以我得走啦，再见，先生。祝你也能玩得开心！
t_en: I have to go, goodbye Mister. Hope you have a good time too!

t_ch: 深不见底，就和匹诺康尼一样，对吧？
t_en: Only on Penacony, am I right?

t_ch: 你怎么还在？
t_en: What are you still doing here?

t_ch: 你早就清楚，如果家族真对每一位前来求援的家人都投以宽容，又何必这样高垒深堑？
t_en: You've known all along, if The Family truly showed leniency to everyone who came seeking help, why would there be a need for such high walls and deep moats?

t_ch: 但人们不这么想，毕竟美梦糖浆的味道实在诱人。你在匹诺康尼孤立无援，只能凭一己之力扳倒高墙…怎么可能？
t_en: However, people don't see it like that, especially since the flavor of the dream syrup is so alluring. You will receive no charity on Penacony, and relying on your own strength alone to topple high walls...? Not a likely feat.

t_ch: 所以一踏进酒店，你就摘下高高的帽子，开始四处求人，像极了一条在沙漠里捡食的鬣狗。因为你知道，机会稍纵即逝。
t_en: Which is why once you step into the hotel, you remove your high hat and beg everyone you come across for help, like a hyena scavenging for scraps in the desert. Because you know that opportunities are fleeting.

t_ch: 跟你的说法相比，拉帝奥的「阿蒂尼孔雀」都显得动听极了。
t_en: When you put it like that, even Ratio's "Attini Peacock" analogy sounds pleasant.

t_ch: 你知道我很少说真心话，劝你把它听进去。
t_en: You know how rare it is for me to give you the straight dope, so listen while you can.

t_ch: 正好，你提到了那位教授——我特别喜欢你和他的共同点，阴谋和算计…尤其是结局的那部分，一场华丽的背叛！
t_en: It's good timing that you mentioned the doctor. I'm especially fond of what you and he have in common, the conspiracies... calculations... especially the part about the finale, a magnificent act of betrayal!

t_ch: …当所有人都这么以为的时候，谁又会去怀疑，那是你精心设下的又一场圈套呢？
t_en: ...When everyone thinks this way, who would even suspect that it was another trap you've meticulously devised?

t_ch: 我说对了么？你就是这样的人，谨小慎微又妄自菲薄，赢了这么多，却还是比谁都怕输。
t_en: Go on. Tell me I'm right. You know who you really are, Mr. Cavalier Gambler: Uptight, overcautious, massive inferiority complex. You've won so much, and you're still SO afraid of losing.

t_ch: 人们只看见你在牌局上一掷千金，却不知道在牌桌下还有另一只手，握紧筹码，颤抖不已……
t_en: They only see your big bets, your bravado, the full house, the straight flush. They don't know the other hand is below the table, clutching your chips for dear life.

t_ch: 厉害啊，难怪酒馆会给你发邀请。你天生就是个好演员…不光擅长骗别人，更擅长骗自己。
t_en: It's a heck of an act. No wonder the tavern sent you an invite. You're a natural, kid. You don't stop at fooling the audience — you fool yourself too.

t_ch: 要想让自己不在人面前露馅，最高明的办法就是先骗过自己。
t_en: The best way to prevent others from seeing your true colors, is first being able to fool yourself.

t_ch: 哈哈，当然，我太了解你了…不过，真奇怪，为什么你拒绝了那份邀请？明明你有过拥抱「欢愉」的机会，那不是你最想要的吗？可你还是选择了公司的牌桌……
t_en: Haha, of course, I know you all too well... But, it's strange — why did you decline that invitation? You had the chance to embrace Elation. Was that not what you most wanted? But you chose the IPC instead...

t_ch: 为了「存护」？哼，我看不像。你和「存护」有半点关系吗？
t_en: For the Preservation? Hmph, I doubt it. Do you even have anything in common with the Preservation?

t_ch: 我以为你知道呢…你不是很懂我么？
t_en: I thought you knew... Didn't you say you had me pegged?

t_ch: 行了，要么现在闭上嘴，要么赶紧从我眼前消失。
t_en: We're done. Either stop talking, or disappear from my sight.

t_ch: 没问题。不过，即将在这里消失的——到底是谁呢？
t_en: That's fine. But, who exactly is about to disappear here?

t_ch: 啊——要玩捉迷藏吗？我最擅长这个了——
t_en: Ahh, playing hide-and-seek? I'm really good at this!

t_ch: 捉迷藏…真是甜美的童年回忆。
t_en: Hide-and-seek... What sweet childhood memories.

t_ch: 和妈妈告别的那天，有多少卡提卡人在屁股后边像豺狼一样追着你们？
t_en: When Mama said goodbye that day, how many Katicans were like jackals hot on your heels?

t_ch: 我打赌你肯定忘不了他们尖利的笑声。为了让自己从那帮野蛮人的鼻子底下消失，你和姐姐只能在血水里打滚，毁了爸爸留下的唯一一件衣服……
t_en: I know you won't forget that sound anytime soon, those shrill cackles? You had to hide right under the noses of those savages, you and Big Sis, playing dead, drifting in all that bloody water. Completely ruined that shirt. Shame. Wasn't that the last one Dad left behind?

t_ch: 它没有被毁掉，我一直保存着。
t_en: It wasn't ruined. I've always kept it.

t_ch: 那只是块破布，你再也穿不上了。
t_en: Come on. It's a rag. It's not like you can ever wear it.

t_ch: 现在你也不用躲躲藏藏了，甚至还有心情嫌弃自己娇贵的行头被雨水打湿…到底是身份变了啊。
t_en: Now you don't have to hide. You probably won't even deign to get your pretty outfit wet in the rain... Your social capital has changed, after all.

t_ch: …我从来没变过。
t_en: ...I've never changed.

t_ch: 不，你变了…你现在变成追人的那个了。最后一次捉迷藏…好好享受吧。
t_en: On the contrary, now you're the one who does the hunting. The last round of hide and seek, and you get to be it. You should enjoy it.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: Is this...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「托帕石」</color>？为什么会在这里……
t_en: A <color=#dbc291ff>topaz</color>? What is this doing here...
Uhh...

t_ch: 怎么，她的基石就这么让你撕心裂肺？
t_en: What, does her cornerstone wrench at your heart so?

t_ch: …我只是好奇它为什么在这里罢了。
t_en: ...I was merely curious why it was here.

t_ch: 兴许是那个翅膀头为了嘲讽你才故意放这的。好让你明白，你费劲布置的魔术大秀不过是垂死挣扎。
t_en: Maybe that winged guy put it here to taunt you. Just to make you realize that your painstakingly arranged magic show is nothing but a death rattle.

t_ch: 「基石的色泽和克里珀圣体的光芒如出一辙」…亏你能编出这种荒唐的借口。但凡他多长个心眼，你的谎言便一触即溃。
t_en: "The cornerstone's hue is the same as the radiance of Qlipoth's body"... I've got to give it to you, I've heard a lot of baloney, but that lie deserves a prize for sheer nerve. If he were just a little bit smarter, the jig would've been up right there.

t_ch: 这只是个诱饵。
t_en: This is just bait.

t_ch: 当然！所以你才会把拉帝奥的「背叛」也列为计划中关键的一环。不得不说，那位教授的演技十分逼真。
t_en: Of course! That's why Ratio's betrayal was one of the keys to your plan. I have to say, that doctor's acting was superb.

t_ch: 也许他根本没有在演呢？
t_en: Or maybe he wasn't acting at all?

t_ch: 哈，那岂不是更正中你的下怀？
t_en: All the better for you!

t_ch: 那位一丝不苟的{RUBY_B#星期日}橡木家主{RUBY_E#}有着近乎偏执的控制欲，你必须让他掌握足够多的信息，又不能让他察觉到破绽……
t_en: Sunday didn't become head of the Oak Family by acting sloppy. He's obsessed with control. You have to give him enough detail to satisfy his meticulous nature, but not so much that he gets suspicious.

t_ch: …所以你让拉帝奥找到他，故意把计划泄露出去。为了不让对方起疑，你向拉帝奥交代的内容全都是真实的，他也如实转告了星期日。
t_en: ...Which is why you had Ratio seek him out and leak the plan on purpose — to prevent the other party from suspecting anything. The intel you gave to Ratio was all true, and he spoke the same to Sunday.

t_ch: 最后，这位家主通过你布下的{RUBY_B#托帕石}「诱饵」{RUBY_E#}，如愿以偿找到了另一块基石，如此一来——
t_en: Finally, Sunday took {RUBY_B#topaz}the bait{RUBY_E#}, found the other cornerstone, and before you know it...

t_ch: ——你才能将{RUBY_B#砂金石}第三块石头{RUBY_E#}瞒天过海。
t_en: ...Everyone's distracted enough for you to steal {RUBY_B#aventurine}the third stone{RUBY_E#}, right out from under them.

t_ch: …别在我脑子里乱翻了，混蛋。
t_en: ...Why don't you stop rummaging through my mind.

t_ch: 你的脑子？是我们的脑子。你就是我，我就是你，不分彼此。
t_en: Your mind? It's our mind. You're me, and I'm you. We're the same.

t_ch: 「要想让自己不在人前露馅，最高明的办法就是先骗过自己」…要我说，你根本骗不了自己。这次能得手算你走运。
t_en: "The best way to prevent others from seeing your true colors, is to first be able to fool yourself"... Really? You can't even fool yourself. You just got lucky this time.

t_ch: 这是星期日手里的另一块基石。相当漂亮的绿色，就像你一样，圆滑、狡诈……
t_en: This is the other cornerstone in Sunday's hands. A rather beautiful green, just like you, smooth and cunning...

t_ch: 告诉我，它叫什么名字？
t_en: Tell me, what's its name?

t_ch: 何必特意问我？
t_en: Why are you even asking me?

t_ch: 哼…那我就亲自让你回忆一下。「砂金」是幸运与诡计之石…你拿到这块石头的时候，她是这么说的，对吧？
t_en: Ugh, must I do all the work? Aventurine is the stone of luck and trickery... That's what she said when you received the stone, wasn't it?

t_ch: 这种石头并不珍稀，但色泽却与某种宝石十分接近，因而常被用作后者的{RUBY_B#伪装}替代{RUBY_E#}。而那种更昂贵的宝石……
t_en: This type of stone isn't rare, but its hue is very similar to a certain gem. In fact, it's often used as {RUBY_B#counterfeit}a substitute{RUBY_E#}. And that more precious gem is...

t_ch: …叫做<color=#dbc291ff>「翡翠」</color>。
t_en: ...<color=#dbc291ff>Jade</color>.

t_ch: 就连那位星期日也没能分辨出来，看来翡翠也并非不能替代砂金。
t_en: Even Sunday can't tell the difference. It looks like jade can be substituted for aventurine, too. Sauce for the goose...

t_ch: 砂金、托帕、翡翠…三位{RUBY_B#石心十人}精英{RUBY_E#}，三块基石，为了小小的匹诺康尼，不惜押上自己的全部。你们比家族还团结一心啊。
t_en: Aventurine, Topaz, Jade... Three {RUBY_B#Ten Stonehearts}elites{RUBY_E#}, three cornerstones who, for a measly Penacony, offered their everything. You're even more united than The Family.

t_ch: 我早说过，三枚「筹码」足矣——所有，或者一无所有。
t_en: As I said before, three chips are sufficient — all, or nothing.

t_ch: 究竟是前者还是后者？哈，我们很快就能见真章了。
t_en: But will it be the former, or the latter? Hah, we'll find out soon enough.

t_ch: 所以，真正的「砂金石」呢？拿出来看看吧。
t_en: So, where's the real aventurine stone? Take it out. Let's have a look.

t_ch: 呵，你又不知道它在哪儿了？
t_en: Heh, suddenly you don't know where it is?

t_ch: 我只是要你亲口说出来…毕竟它如今的样子真的和主人很相称。
t_en: I just want to hear you say it... After all, it really does resemble its owner.

t_ch: …那我就如你所愿。*它们*一直待在最适合的地方，从未离开……
t_en: ...As you wish then. They never went anywhere. They're right where they belong...

t_ch: …就在这堆廉价的珠宝里。
t_en: Piled up with these cheap baubles.

t_ch: 你在出发前，就把「砂金石」砸碎了……
t_en: You smashed the aventurine stone before you left...?

t_ch: …看它的样子，啧啧，多像你支离破碎的人生啊。再怎么披着光鲜亮丽的外表，里头依旧是颗卑微的小石子…这玩意可比你的命重要得多。
t_en: ...Just look at it, tsk. Shattered, just like your life. Poor thing. A humble pebble coated in the most lustrous sheen. I take it back, this thing is far more precious than your life.

t_ch: 这么做的下场你再清楚不过，亵渎{RUBY_B#基石}克里珀圣体{RUBY_E#}之人，你觉得公司会放过你？
t_en: You're absolutely clear about the consequences of doing this? Blasphemy against {RUBY_B#Cornerstone}Qlipoth's body{RUBY_E#}. You think the IPC will let you get off scot-free?

t_ch: 「钻石」向来看重结果。只要我能创造的价值远高于成本…过程和手段就不是问题。
t_en: Diamond has always been all about results. As long as I can create value far beyond the cost, the ends justify the means.

t_ch: 不付出一点代价，怎么能骗过家族？没关系，「存护」的基石就算裂成碎片也能发挥作用，尽管效用会大打折扣，但对我来说…足够了。
t_en: How else would The Family be fooled if there was no price to pay? It doesn't matter — even smashed to smithereens, the Preservation's cornerstone can still be used. Its effect may be greatly diminished, but... it's enough for me.

t_ch: 我真的有点好奇了。为什么你迈出的每一步都在铤而走险，为自己准备的选项永远伴随强烈的自毁冲动？
t_en: Now I'm really curious. Why does every step you take involve reckless risks, and the choices you prepare for yourself always come with a strong impulse for self-destruction?

t_ch: 难道你真的相信「风险越大，回报越高」？看不出你对公司如此忠诚啊。
t_en: Do you truly believe that the greater the risk, the greater the rewards? I wouldn't have guessed you'd be so loyal to the IPC.

t_ch: 哼，你看不出的事情多了去了…所以你也看不出我要如何赢得一切。
t_en: Hmph, there is so much you don't see... Which is also why you'll never see how I'll win it all.

t_ch: 前提是你真能做到。
t_en: That is, if you can do what it takes.

t_ch: …我们拭目以待。
t_en: ...We'll just have to wait and see then.

t_ch: 你好，我们又见面了，眼睛很漂亮的先生。
t_en: Hello, we meet again, Mr. Pretty-eyes.

t_ch: …是啊，又见面了。你找到爸爸妈妈了么？
t_en: ...Yes, we meet again. Did you find your mother and father?

t_ch: 当然，姐姐也在，我们四个人刚玩过捉迷藏。
t_en: Of course, Big Sis is there too. The four of us were just playing hide-and-seek.

t_ch: 真开心呀，来这儿的路上，爸爸还带我见识了*蕉皮电影*。
t_en: I'm so happy! On our way here, Papa even brought me to see a flim.

t_ch: 你想说「胶片电影」吧。
t_en: I think you mean "film."

t_ch: 对，就是这个。把很多很多纸版画放在一起，就变成了会动的壁画。
t_en: Yes, that's it. Putting many drawings together and turning them into a moving wall painting.

t_ch: 把我和爸爸，妈妈，还有姐姐放在一起，就变成了一家人。
t_en: They put me, Papa, Mama and Big Sis together, turning us into one big family.

t_ch: 你也来试试吧，先生！看你一直愁眉苦脸的，在游乐园要开心点呀。
t_en: You should give it a try too, Mister! You look sad. The amusement park will cheer you up.
...Sure.

t_ch: 快去试试吧，先生！
t_en: Go give it a try, Mister!

t_ch: 怎么样，这分数不低吧？
t_en: How's that? Not a bad score, eh?

t_ch: …没意思。
t_en: ...Boring.

t_ch: 怎么不说话了？
t_en: Why aren't you talking?

t_ch: 你确实勾起了我的兴趣…我承认你身上还有些我不完全了解的地方。
t_en: You've piqued my interest... I'll admit that there are still aspects of you that I don't completely understand.

t_ch: 你这次倒是很真诚。
t_en: You sound sincere this time, at least.

t_ch: 真诚是我为数不多的宝贵品质，不用特意强调。看见那片迷宫了么？在你走出那里前，我就能彻底了解你了。
t_en: Sincerity is one of my traits that everybody likes. And I have precious few of those. Moving on! See that maze over there? I'll know everything about you before you reach the exit.

t_ch: 我们的影城之旅尚未结束，你的走马灯也在继续…而我不介意将这一过程拉得很长很长。
t_en: Our quaint journey through this amusement park still isn't over... and I wouldn't mind stretching this out at all.

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: Is this...

t_ch: 想起什么了？
t_en: What's on your mind?

t_ch: 和你没关系。
t_en: It's got nothing to do with you.

t_ch: 需要一点提示么？这是一副镣铐，套在你身上的。那男人给你的第一份「工作」，你挣到的第一桶金…我可记得清清楚楚。
t_en: Do you need a hint? These are manacles on you. That man gave you your first job, and you made your first pot of gold... I remember it all too well.

t_ch: 你就是把这捆铁链缠在拳头上，那是你能找到的唯一一件{RUBY_B#武器}工具{RUBY_E#}。然后，在那座迷宫里，你……
t_en: You wrapped that iron chain around your fist, for that is the only {RUBY_B#weapon}tool{RUBY_E#} you could find. Then, in that maze, you...

t_ch: …闭嘴吧。
t_en: ...Shut up.

t_ch: 哦…你不愿面对那段过往？不想承认你这条命只值六十个{RUBY_B#赤铜币}塔安巴{RUBY_E#}？
t_en: Oh... You don't wish to face your past? Unwilling to admit that your life is worth only sixty {RUBY_B#Red Copper Coins}Tanbas{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 依我看，两者都不是正确答案…你拒绝面对它，只因为它证明了你的软弱。
t_en: From what I see, you refuse to confront it, because it only proves your weakness.

t_ch: 软弱的人怎么会铤而走险？
t_en: How could a weak person take such daring risks?

t_ch: 不错，你是喜欢铤而走险…却偏偏不肯放下某些多余的东西。就算在这片美梦中，你也只敢在自己身上尝试*死亡*。
t_en: That's right. You love the thrill of danger... but you refuse to let go of meaningless sentiments. Even in this beautiful dream, the only thing you "dare" allow yourself is death.

t_ch: 那些随行人员本可以成为你手上的鬼牌，发挥更大的用处。家族的污点要多少有多少，只需做出一些小小的牺牲…换成「欧泊」，早迎刃而解了。
t_en: In your hands, those who follow you could've become joker cards. They're far more useful that way. It's not like this is the only place The Family ever cut corners. You could've had tons of action if you weren't so all-or-nothing. All it takes is a meager sacrifice... I bet "Opal" would have resolved this without a fuss.

t_ch: 可惜，你不如他。但凡你做了，也不会沦落至此…为什么不这么做呢？应该不是出于什么职业道德吧？
t_en: A pity you're not him. You wouldn't be in this state if you could just get with the program.... And why couldn't you? Out of professional integrity?

t_ch: 你说的那些{RUBY_B#千术}技巧{RUBY_E#}效率是很高，但我不是不会，而是不屑用，懂么？
t_en: Those {RUBY_B#cheats}techniques{RUBY_E#} you mentioned are highly efficient, but it's not that I don't know about them — it's more like I couldn't care less. Get it?

t_ch: 如果对局不公平，还有什么乐趣可言？
t_en: What fun is it if the fight isn't fair?

t_ch: 「公平」…呵，好像你的对手对你多公平似的。局势明明对你不利，你为什么还能这么游刃有余？那假面愚者的话究竟点醒了你什么？
t_en: Fair... Heh, you assume your opponents would fight fair in the first place. The odds are obviously not in your favor, so how are you just breezing by? Did that Masked Fool's words awaken something in you?

t_ch: 她给了我一个足以颠覆一切的答案。
t_en: She gave me an answer that could turn everything upside down.

t_ch: 哼，颠覆一切？
t_en: Hmph, everything?

t_ch: 你是说——让牌桌上的一切都消失吗？
t_en: You mean, it could make all the cards on the table just... disappear?

t_ch: …这是作弊。
t_en: ...That's cheating.

t_ch: 你现在的表情真的很难形容。妈妈给你留下的这枚护身符是纯金打造的，为什么从没考虑过卖了它？
t_en: Your expression right now is hard to put into words. The lucky charm Mama left you is made from gold. Why did you never consider selling it?

t_ch: 明明那样你就能和姐姐过上一阵子正常人的生活了。回过头看，那才是更好的选择。
t_en: You obviously could've lived a normal life like Big Sis that way. Looking back, that was the better choice.

t_ch: 妈妈只留给我们两件首饰：一条项链，一枚护身符。不会再有第三件了。
t_en: Mama only left us with two pieces of jewelry: A necklace and a lucky charm. There won't ever be a third piece.

t_ch: 你一直是这么说的——但其实你很后悔吧？没有卖掉它们？
t_en: That's what you always say, but you actually regret it, don't you? That you didn't sell them?

t_ch: 别没话找话。
t_en: You can zip it if there's nothing to talk about.

t_ch: 哈…我知道了。你一定记得姐姐当时说过的话：「你是受母神赐福的孩子，你能带领氏族走向幸福。所以永远记得保护好自己，也永远不要怨恨痛苦和贫穷。」
t_en: Ha... I know. You must remember what Big Sis told you: You're a child blessed by Gaiathra Triclops and can lead the clan to happiness. So, always remember to protect yourself and never resent the pain and poverty you're going through...

t_ch: 言犹在耳，对吧？你是个乖孩子，绝不会忘记。
t_en: The words still ring in your ears, don't they? You're a good kid, so you definitely won't have forgotten.

t_ch: 所以你也一定不会忘记，她生命最后一刻是如何凄惨，你身后的声声尖笑又是如何钻心…你就那样头也不回地逃走了，照她说的做了。
t_en: So you surely won't forget how tragic her last moments of life were, how the piercing laughter behind you felt like it was drilling into your heart... You ran away without looking back, just as she told you to.

t_ch: 啧啧…抱憾终身啊。
t_en: Tsk tsk... A lifelong regret, indeed.

t_ch: 真是够了…你就没有别的话题可聊吗？
t_en: Enough... Do you not have anything better to talk about?

t_ch: 这是你第二次打断我了。你真的很好懂。
t_en: Oh! Shut me down like a champ. Well I guess when it comes to mind games this isn't exactly your first rodeo.

t_ch: 我终于能明白你的想法了…哼，真是疯狂啊。
t_en: I think I finally get you. And *whew* you are nuts.

t_ch: 「最后，我会颠覆这场美梦，创造<color=#dbc291ff>最盛大的死亡</color>」——这的确是你的主张，贯彻始终，从未改变。
t_en: "In the end, I'll ruin this beautiful dream and create <color=#dbc291ff>the grandest death</color>." — You've stuck to that, start to finish.

t_ch: 从那颗星核，到知更鸟的失声、两起命案、与星期日的交涉，再到那假面愚者的提示，只有这不变的两个字能勾起你的兴趣。而现在，你确实将它握在了手中……
t_en: Think about it. There's a Stellaron in play, the fabulous Robin loses her voice, two unsolved murders, cryptic messages from a Masked Fool, and a chance to go head-to-head with Sunday himself... and the only thing to pique your interest is one word — the last word, a word that's right there at your fingertips...

t_ch: …但那究竟是谁的「死亡」？
t_en: ..."Death," but whose, exactly?

t_ch: 等骰子落定我们就知道了。
t_en: We'll know when the dice falls.

t_ch: 好啊，那观众席给我留个位置，我倒要看看你有多大本事。不过，我还是很好奇……
t_en: *laugh* Alright then, reserve a seat for me in the audience. I'm curious to see just how capable you are. Still, you never answered my question:

t_ch: 如果一切从头来过…你还想当被母神赐福的孩子么？
t_en: If you could start over... would you still want to be the child who received Gaiathra's blessing?

t_ch: 不知道为什么，先生，你总给我一种特别的感觉…让我很好奇，跃跃欲试。
t_en: I don't know why, Mister, but you always give me a special feeling... that makes me more curious about you.

t_ch: 可惜没能再多认识你一些。我们该告别了，你玩得还开心吗？
t_en: It's sad that I can't get to know you more. We have to say goodbye. Did you have fun?

t_ch: 你…要回去了吗？
t_en: You're... going back?

t_ch: 嗯，我该回家了。天色开始阴沉，要下雨了，我不能让大家担心。
t_en: Yes, I should go home. The day's getting dark, and it's going to rain. I don't want to worry the others.

t_ch: 你的家…在哪里？
t_en: Your home... Where is it?

t_ch: 真是个怪问题。家就是有爸爸、妈妈、姐姐在的地方……
t_en: What a strange question. It's where Papa, Mama and Big Sis are...

t_ch: …就在这片梦里。
t_en: ...In this dream.

t_ch: 这座游乐园，这片美梦，真的很安详。所有人都喜欢它。
t_en: This amusement park, this beautiful dream, they really are peaceful. Everyone loves it.

t_ch: 可是先生，为什么你不喜欢？
t_en: But Mister, why don't you like it?

t_ch: …因为他们不在这里。
t_en: ...Because they're not here.

t_ch: 那他们在哪里？
t_en: Where are they then?

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 你明明知道。只是答案没有意义。
t_en: You DO know. But there's no point pressing the issue.

t_ch: 承认吧，你累了。
t_en: Admit it. You're tired.

t_ch: 我们都累了，所以打算留在这里，我…还有他。
t_en: We all are, which is why we chose to stay here. Me... and him.

t_ch: 你的「过去」…和「未来」。
t_en: Your past... and future.

t_ch: 留在这里是多久？
t_en: How long will you stay?

t_ch: 永远。我们会和你一起，永远留在这片梦中。
t_en: Forever. We'll be with you, forever in this dream.

t_ch: …这是我们为决意赴死之人献上的最大敬意。
t_en: ...This is the greatest honor that we can offer to those who hurtle towards death.

t_ch: 剑走偏锋，那是一种极为荒诞的做法，但在你身上并不罕见。因为「自己的生命」向来是最先被你扔出的那枚筹码，一直如此。
t_en: The road less traveled is less traveled for a reason. But you've never gone in any other direction. Your own life is the chip you're most eager to lay down. Always has been.

t_ch: 你并不关心真凶是谁，对所谓的遗产也不感兴趣。你只想当好一个秉公办事的公司职员，在家族的地盘处处受难，被戴上滚烫的镣铐，推向舞台中央……
t_en: You don't care who the real murderer is. And the Watchmaker's so-called "legacy" couldn't be more boring. What you want, what you need, is to be the smooth operator — the solid gold deal-maker who doesn't waste a drop of sweat even when he's up to his neck in danger, deep inside Family territory. You want to be polished up, cuffed with red-hot chains and spotlit center stage...

t_ch: 然后，成为这场盛会的「第三个牺牲品」。
t_en: You'll be the closing act! The final sacrifice.

t_ch: …我可以做到，并且天衣无缝。
t_en: ...I can do it, and it will be flawless.

t_ch: 你当然能。你的好运一定会在恰到好处的时候帮你，一枚<color=#dbc291ff>星核</color>和一位<color=#dbc291ff>令使</color>…就这么简单，对吗？
t_en: Of course you can. Your luck will help you at the right time — a <color=#dbc291ff>Stellaron</color> and an <color=#dbc291ff>Emanator</color>... It's that simple, no?

t_ch: 如此一来，公司便能获得上桌的资格。而你也能从无尽的漩涡中抽身，得到梦寐以求的解脱。
t_en: That way, the IPC will earn a place at the table. And as for you, you'll just happen to slip the leash, leave the rat race, extract yourself from this endless debacle. You'll have the freedom you've always dreamed of.

t_ch: 已经不是第一次了…这是你最擅长，也最渴望的方式。这场闹剧以一场「死亡」开始，也将在一场「死亡」中落幕。
t_en: This isn't the first time... you've been an escape artist from day one. This fiasco started with a "Death," and its curtains will fall on another "Death."

t_ch: …所以「钻石」才会选择你？
t_en: ...So, that's why Diamond chose you?

t_ch: 他要的只有匹诺康尼，无论手段，不计代价…也和具体的人无关。
t_en: He's just after Penacony, no matter the means, no matter the price... It's not personal.

t_ch: 很辛苦吧。
t_en: It's hard, isn't it?

t_ch: 你忽然变得很体贴啊，良心发现了？
t_en: Don't get soft on me now. What, did you suddenly grow a conscience?

t_ch: 我终究是从你的自我中诞生的，所以我很清楚，想取回掷出的筹码难如登天。
t_en: Hey, I was born from your self. I'm well aware that climbing out of the hole you've dug is basically impossible.

t_ch: 你要做的事我阻止不了，你要去往的地方…我们也改变不了。
t_en: I can't stop you from doing what you want, and... we can't change where you want to go.

t_ch: 覆水难收。我们能做的只有抓住每个机会，让自己尽可能多赢一秒。
t_en: What's done can't be undone. All we can do is play the cards we're dealt, and rake in as much time as possible.

t_ch: 是啊。可惜人不可能一辈子只做正确的决定…虽然好运总是站在你这边。
t_en: Yes. Alas, people won't make all the right choices in their lifetime... Though luck always seems like it's on your side.

t_ch: 你总会赢下去，你从未输过——但为什么是你？为什么…非得是你？
t_en: You will keep winning, having never lost before. But why you? Why... must it be you?

t_ch: 如果一个幸运儿的奇迹，全部建立在所有他所爱之人、甚至更多人的不幸之上…如果你带来的每一场雨从不象征母神的宽恕和恩赐，而是一次又一次无意义的死亡……
t_en: If all your luck is built on the pain of someone you love, on the loss of dozens more — if these windfalls, these jackpots aren't a gift from Gaiathra — if all they are is a long string of meaningless deaths...

t_ch: …那你我究竟是犯了多少错误，才要出生在这世上？
t_en: ...Then what did we do to deserve living in a world like that?

t_ch: …也许等我抵达了那个终点，我们的困惑就能得到解答了。
t_en: Maybe... maybe when I get to where I'm going and look back, I'll know what the trip was all about.

t_ch: 哼…好吧。先走一步，朋友，我在{RUBY_B#「未来」}前面{RUBY_E#}等你。
t_en: Hmph... Fine. Time to make a move, my friend. I'll be waiting up {RUBY_B#Future}ahead{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 最后的时光，同{RUBY_B#「过去」}这孩子{RUBY_E#}好好道个别吧。尽量让自己……
t_en: Say goodbye to {RUBY_B#Past}the kid{RUBY_E#} before you shuffle off. It's...

t_ch: …死而无憾。
t_en: It's best to die without regrets.

t_ch: 这下…就剩我们了。
t_en: And now... only we are left.

t_ch: 可以为我拍张照吗？就当是留个纪念。
t_en: Can you take a photo for me? I want a memento.

t_ch: …嗯，来吧。
t_en: ...Sure, come on.

t_ch: 可以为我拍张照吗？就当是留个纪念。
t_en: Can you take a photo for me? I want a memento.

t_ch: 真好，这样我也能看见自己的样子了。
t_en: How nice! Now I can see what I look like too.

t_ch: 下次拍照时，记得看镜头，表情会更自然些。
t_en: Look at the lens when you're taking a photo the next time — your expression will look more natural.

t_ch: 嗯，我会的。那…先生，你也要回去了吗？
t_en: Sure, I will. Then... Mister, are you going back too?

t_ch: 我还不能走。
t_en: I can't leave yet.

t_ch: 我在这里还有一场…表演。
t_en: I still have... a show to do.

t_ch: 哦…那你马上要登台演出了，是吗？
t_en: Oh... You're about to go on stage, aren't you?

t_ch: 那走吧，我送你去大舞台那里。
t_en: Let's go then. I'll take you to the stage.
...Sure.

t_ch: 这道幕布后就是大舞台了……
t_en: Behind this curtain is the grand stage...

t_ch: 马上就是登台的时间了，你做好准备了吗？祝你的演出圆满成功。
t_en: It's almost time to go on stage. Are you ready? Good luck with your show.

t_ch: 谢谢你。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 你看起来还是很紧张……
t_en: You still seem pretty nervous...

t_ch: 那我们来「对掌」吧。如果有母神保佑，你就可以轻松点了。
t_en: Let's put our palms together. If you receive Gaiathra's blessing, you'll feel more relaxed.

t_ch: 「对掌」是一种小小的仪式，我们把手掌贴在一起，把祷文念给芬戈妈妈听，她就会祝福我们。
t_en: Putting our palms together is a simple ritual. By putting our palms together and reciting the prayer to Mama Fenge, she will bless us.

t_ch: 如果你不会，我可以教你。
t_en: If you are not familiar, I can guide you.

t_ch: 没关系，我会的。
t_en: It's alright. I know how to do it.

t_ch: 我当然会。
t_en: Of course I know.

t_ch: 我们得在这里分别了，卡卡瓦夏。卡提卡人就要来了。
t_en: This is where we go our own way, Kakavasha. The Katicans are coming.

t_ch: 为什么？卡提卡人已经抢走了我们所有的钱、吃的，还杀死了爸爸妈妈…他们还想要什么？
t_en: Why? The Katicans have already taken all our money, food, and they killed our parents... What more do they want?

t_ch: 卡提卡人嗜血、残忍，贪得无厌。他们想要一切，所以他们什么也得不到。
t_en: Katicans are bloodthirsty, cruel, and insatiably greedy. They want everything, only to end up with nothing.

t_ch: 这是个诡计，一场复仇。记得吗？今天是「卡卡瓦」之日，也是你的生日。
t_en: This is a trick, an act of revenge. Remember? Today is the day of the Kakava, and also your birthday.

t_ch: 他们知道埃维金人一定会在这天举办祭典。借着这场雨，他们会来摧毁我们的大篷车，抢走想要的一切。
t_en: They know the Avgin will surely hold a festival today. With the aid of this rain, they will come to destroy our wagons and take everything they want.

t_ch: 但卡提卡人不知道，这次我们会反抗，天上来的黑衣人也站在我们这边。卡提卡人在他们面前毫无胜算，一定会为自己的傲慢付出代价。
t_en: Little do the Katicans know, this time we will fight back! The men in black that descend from the skies are on our side. The Katicans stand no chance against them and will surely pay for their arrogance.

t_ch: 如果没有这场雨，卡提卡人就不会行动，我们也没有机会周旋。这都是母神的恩赐，而你是{RUBY_B#受母神赐福的孩子}「卡卡瓦夏」{RUBY_E#}，你的好运会保佑姐姐成功。
t_en: Without this rain, the Katicans would never take action, and we would not have the chance to turn the tide. This is a gift from Gaiathra, and you are {RUBY_B#Child Blessed by Gaiathra}Kakavasha{RUBY_E#}, whose good fortune will bless your sister with success.

t_ch: 可…可有人会死掉的，你也会有危险…这哪里是好运了！你们为什么要这么做……
t_en: But... but people will die, and you will be in danger... How is that good fortune? Why are you all doing this...

t_ch: 埃维金人有仇必报。母神在呼唤我，爸爸妈妈在等我，我必须回应。但她将好运赐给你，要你活下去。
t_en: The Avgin always return their blood debts. Gaiathra calls for me, while Papa and Mama are waiting for me. I must answer the call. But she will bless you with good fortune and help you survive.

t_ch: 只要你还活着，埃维金人的血就永远不会流干。所以跑吧，卡卡瓦夏，不要害怕，不要回头。到山的那一边去。雨会长伴你，雨会保佑你。
t_en: As long as you are alive, the blood of the Avgin will never run dry. So run, Kakavasha, do not be afraid, and do not look back. Go to the other side of the mountain. The rain will accompany you, and the rain will bless you.

t_ch: 而我们，将在下一次{RUBY_B#母神轮回之始}「卡卡瓦」{RUBY_E#}的极光下重逢……
t_en: As for us, we will reunite in {RUBY_B#Start of Gaiathra Reincarnation}Kakava{RUBY_E#}'s next aurora.

t_ch: 「愿母神三度为你阖眼……」
t_en: "May the goddess Gaiathra close her eyes three times..."

t_ch: 「令你的血脉永远鼓动……」
t_en: "Keep your blood eternally pulsing..."

t_ch: 「旅途永远坦然……」
t_en: "Let your journey be forever peaceful... "

t_ch: 「…诡计永不败露。」
t_en: "...and your schemes forever concealed."

t_ch: 再见，卡卡瓦夏。
t_en: Farewell, Kakavasha.

t_ch: 「星际和平播报快讯：公司市场开拓部发言人证实无主星区茨冈尼亚爆发小规模叛乱，目前局势已得到有效控制——」
t_en: "Newsflash from the Interastral Peace Broadcast: The IPC Marketing Development Department's spokesperson confirms that a small-scale rebellion has broken out in the unclaimed region of Sigonia. The situation is now under control..."

t_ch: 「叛乱分子来自当地一支名为『卡提卡』的氏族，该氏族长期对公司心怀不满，对市场开拓部在当地的工作产生了严重的负面影响——」
t_en: "The insurgents are from a local clan known as Katica, which has a long history of disdain towards the IPC. This has caused a serious negative impact to the work of the IPC's Marketing Development Department..."

t_ch: 「该氏族向处于公司保护下的『埃维金』氏族发起大规模袭击，造成<unbreak>6728</unbreak>人死亡，<unbreak>3452</unbreak>人失踪，伤者目前已被医疗救援组织『创伤战线』接收——」
t_en: "The clan launched a massive attack on the Avgin, who were under the protection of the IPC, resulting in <unbreak>6,728</unbreak> deaths and <unbreak>3,452</unbreak> missing. The casualties are currently under the care of the Frontline Trauma Team..."

t_ch: 「发言人对这一『深重的人道主义灾难』表达了深切哀悼，同时就此事向全体星际公民发表重要讲话——」
t_en: "The spokesperson expresses his deepest condolences for this devastating humanitarian disaster, at the same time delivering an important message on this matter to all interplanetary citizens..."

t_ch: 「最后，他表示：『存护的巨锤必将为所有生命落下，无论生死，无论种族，无论思想，以捍卫我等与生具有之基本权利』……」
t_en: "Finally, he proclaims: 'The hammer of Preservation will fall for all beings, regardless of life or death, regardless of race, regardless of ideology, to uphold the basic rights we inherently possess.'"

t_ch: 卡卡瓦夏？
t_en: Kakavasha?

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Bye.

t_ch: 登上舞台。
t_en: Go on stage.

t_ch: 好咯——演员已经就位，好戏该开场了。
t_en: Okay, all the actors are in place. It's time for the show to begin.

t_ch: 这场演出献给你，希望它能为你留下难忘的回忆……
t_en: This act is dedicated to you. I hope it'll be an unforgettable memory for you...

t_ch: …「卡卡瓦夏」。
t_en: ...Kakavasha.

t_ch: 对了，在你临走前，我还有个比较私人的问题。
t_en: By the way, before you go, I have a personal question.

t_ch: 你…真的想要亲手毁灭这个世界吗？
t_en: You... do you truly want to destroy the world with your own hands?

t_ch: 假设——只是个假设——假设我每次掷骰子都有概率掷出这个结果……
t_en: Let's assume — just assuming, now — that every time I roll the dice, there is a possibility of achieving this particular outcome...

t_ch: 那我一定会非常乐意赌一把的。
t_en: Then, I would be quite happy to make that wager.

t_ch: 先是幻听，现在是幻觉——真棒，下一步我是不是该荣升「同谐」令使了？
t_en: First I'm hearing things, and now I'm seeing them. Great! Am I going to be elevated into the Harmony's Emanator next?

t_ch: …这里怎么一个游客都没有，那翅膀头在搞什么？
t_en: ...Why are there no guests here? What's that feather-head doing?

t_ch: 只有一个皮皮西…不对，小孩子？
t_en: Just a Pepeshi... No, a child?

t_ch: 那对眼睛，还有「芬戈妈妈」…不，这不可能……
t_en: Those eyes... and Mama Fenge... No! It can't...

t_ch: 宇宙中不会再有埃维金人了……
t_en: There AREN'T any Avgins left!

t_ch: 爸爸，妈妈——等等我呀——
t_en: Papa, Mama, wait for me...

t_ch: …反正不是我。
t_en: ...It's not going to be me anyway.

t_ch: 现在换我来躲啦——我赌你们肯定找不到我——
t_en: It's my turn to hide now! I bet you guys can't find meeee!

t_ch: 那孩子…会在这里面么？
t_en: That child... Could he be in here?

t_ch: 基石消失了…又是「同谐」的幻觉啊。
t_en: The cornerstone is gone... Another illusion of the Harmony.
...Huh?

t_ch: 哇，这里好高——比沙漠里最高的石头还要高——
t_en: Woah, this place... it's so tall. Even taller than the tallest rocks in the desert...

t_ch: 还有好多花——姐姐，这朵紫色的送给你——
t_en: And there are so many flowers here. Big Sis, this one's for you!

t_ch: 死路…走错了么？
t_en: A dead end... Is this the wrong way?

t_ch: 如果把这些叶子带回去——会开出新的小花吗——
t_en: If these leaves could be brought back... Would they bloom into new flowers?

t_ch: 这次真安静啊，是他终于消失了…还是我快要消失了？
t_en: It's so quiet this time. Is he finally gone...? Or am I the one about to disappear?

t_ch: 终于…走出这里了。
t_en: Finally... made it out of this place.

t_ch: 要回家了吗——可我还不想回去——
t_en: We're going home? But I don't want to go back yet...

t_ch: 这里好开心——我想一直待在这里——
t_en: It's so fun here... I want to stay here forever...

t_ch: 哈努兄弟！你…要去哪儿？
t_en: Brother Hanu! Where... are you going?

t_ch: 他只会去往一个地方——无论是过去、现在，还是将来！
t_en: There's only one place he can be going, whether it's in the past, present or future!

t_ch: 美梦要结束了！美梦永远不会结束！
t_en: Beautiful dream's about to end! The beautiful dream will never end!

t_ch: 先生…是你吗？我听到了皮鞋的声音。
t_en: Mister... Is that you? I hear the sound of leather shoes.

t_ch: 啊…真的是你。
t_en: Ah... It really is you.

t_ch: 原来你是演员…怪不得衣服那么漂亮。
t_en: So you're an actor... No wonder your clothes are so stylish.

t_ch: 其实我是一名…商人。但我确实有场表演。
t_en: I'm actually a... merchant. But I do have a show to do.

t_ch: 你和天上的黑衣人一样？可你没有穿黑色的衣服。
t_en: Are you the same as those men in black in the sky? But you're not wearing black.

t_ch: 普通员工才要穿那种衣服，我的位置…比他们高得多。
t_en: Only ordinary employees have to wear that. My position... is much higher than theirs.

t_ch: 好厉害，希望我也能成为和你一样漂亮的大人。
t_en: Awesome, I hope I can become as good-looking as you when I grow up.

t_ch: 你可以的。
t_en: Of course you can.

t_ch: 你一定会比我更好、更厉害。
t_en: You'll be better, and stronger than me.

t_ch: 这位就是黄泉小姐吧？你好，我是姬子，星穹列车的领航员。
t_en: Is this Miss Acheron? Hello, I'm Himeko, the Astral Express' navigator.

t_ch: 你好，我叫三月七！{NICKNAME}就不介绍了，你肯定认识。
t_en: Hello, I'm March 7th! I'm sure {NICKNAME} needs no introduction, as you definitely know {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 怎么轮到我就跳过了？
t_en: Hey, I still deserve a proper intro!

t_ch: 请多关照，黄泉小姐。
t_en: Pleasure to meet ya, Miss Acheron.

t_ch: 你可以叫我钟表小子。
t_en: You can call me Clockie.

t_ch: 你们好。对于我的出现，各位似乎并不意外。
t_en: Hello. None of you seem surprised by my arrival.

t_ch: 既然瓦尔特决定与你同行，说明他信任你，而我们同样相信他的判断。
t_en: Since Welt has decided to travel with you, it means that he trusts you, and we trust his judgment.

t_ch: 你们的关系真是令人羡慕。
t_en: I envy your close friendships.

t_ch: 黄泉小姐并非危险分子，对星穹列车也没有敌意，砂金先前的指控只是一面之词。
t_en: Miss Acheron here doesn't present a danger, and she's of no threat to the Astral Express. Aventurine's prior accusation was based on nothing more than his own subjectivity.

t_ch: 因此，在继续我们的合作前，他有义务给出一个合理的解释。
t_en: Which is why, before we continue working together, he has a duty to explain himself.

t_ch: 你是想…制造一个三方共同在场的局面么？
t_en: You want to... create a situation where all three parties are present?
